ocr.text: In the fiscal year 2024, Warner Bros Discovery's User Engagement Metrics reveal insightful data across various platforms for
the first and second quarters. For the Streaming platform, total views reached 5M, with an average watch time of 120 minutes
and an engagement rate of 25%. In the case of Linear TV, the total views amounted to 3M, with an average watch time of 60
minutes and an engagement rate of 15%, categorized under Digital Content. Meanwhile, Video on Demand (VOD) recorded
total views of 2M with an average watch time of 90 minutes and an engagement rate of 18%, associated with Social Media.
Furthermore, the total engagements across all platforms reached 1.5M, reflecting an average watch time of 30 minutes and an
engagement rate of 20%, collectively termed Total Engagements. These metrics across the Streaming, Linear TV, VOD, and
Total Engagements platforms provide critical insights into user behavior and engagement with Warner Bros Discovery's media
offerings, highlighting patterns that could inform future strategic decisions in content delivery and audience interaction. The
data indicates that Streaming leads in total views and engagement rate, suggesting a strong preference for this platform among
users, while Linear TV and VOD present opportunities for growth in both viewership and engagement metrics. This
comprehensive overview underscores the importance of analyzing user engagement as a means to enhance content strategy and
optimize viewer satisfaction across all distribution channels.
